name: Golden Apple Pie with Rum Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the sliced apples with brown sugar, flour, spices, and salt in a 9-inch pie dish lined with crust.
2. Flute the edges of the crust. Sprinkle the apples with nuts and rum; dot with butter.
3. Roll out the remaining pastry dough to form 3/4-inch strips. Arrange the strips in a lattice pattern on top of the pie.
4. Bake at 425°F for 50-60 minutes, or until the apples are tender. Cover partway through baking time to prevent over-browning of the crust.
5. Serve with Rum Sauce: Combine sugar, water, cinnamon, cornstarch, and cold water in a saucepan. Bring to a boil, then gradually blend in the remaining ingredients. Cook until thickened, stirring constantly. Stir in rum and serve warm with pie.
